steps.title: Clear the fraction
steps.content:
Start with the given equation.
$$f=\frac{(12x)^2}{5g}$$
Multiply both sides by $5g$.
$$5fg=(12x)^2$$

steps.title: Isolate the variable term
steps.content:
Square the factor inside the parentheses.
$$(12x)^2=144x^2$$
So the equation becomes
$$5fg=144x^2$$
Divide by $144$.
$$x^2=\frac{5fg}{144}$$

steps.title: Take the positive root
steps.content:
The question says the numbers are positive, so use the positive square root.
$$x=\sqrt{\frac{5fg}{144}}=\frac{\sqrt{5fg}}{12}$$
The answer is A.
